raw_consumer_statement:
I paid {$1600.00} to XXXX XXXX on XX/XX/year>. I cancelled the trip and per XXXX 's policy, they issued a Reservation Credit of {$1600.00} to be used for future travel within one year.

When I found out XXXX XXXX was going bankrupt and ceasing all operations, I immediately contacted Citi to dispute the transaction because I would be unable to use the credit.

I filed a chargeback on XX/XX/year>. Citi contacted XXXX on XX/XX/year>. XXXXXXXX XXXX  bankruptcy representatives responded on XX/XX/year>, actively fighting my chargeback even after the airline had permanently shut down. Citi ruled against me on XX/XX/year>, citing XXXXXXXX XXXX cancellation policy. 
Citi 's decision is wrong. XXXXXXXX XXXX  cancellation policy is completely irrelevant because XXXX no longer exists and can not provide any travel services whatsoever. I am being charged {$1600.00} for something I will never receive under any circumstances. No cancellation policy can legally obligate a consumer to pay for services a merchant is permanently incapable of delivering. This is exactly what the Fair Credit Billing Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1666 ) protects against. The DOT has publicly encouraged XXXX passengers in my exact situation to file chargebacks for services not rendered. Citi has failed to protect my consumer rights and I am requesting a full reversal of {$1600.00}.
